sources.title: Public Art: Twins
supports: the Churchill Square artwork uses sunlight sensors, recorded weather patterns and audio electronics
sources.safe_use: Identity and operating principle of the Brighton artwork.

sources.title: The Earth's hum is driven by ocean waves over the continental shelves
supports: continuous seismic normal-mode excitation near 10 mHz
does_not_support: a single local structure as proof of global hum
direct conversion of all ground vibration into useful electrical power
sources.safe_use: Primary anchor for continuous solid-Earth hum.

sources.title: Excitation of Earth's continuous free oscillations by atmosphere-ocean-seafloor coupling
supports: continuous 2-7 mHz free oscillations on days without major earthquakes
sources.safe_use: Coupled source model for background free oscillations.

sources.title: Increasing ocean wave energy observed in Earth's seismic wavefield since the late 20th century
supports: globally observable primary microseisms at approximately 14-20 second periods
ocean-wave energy coupling into solid Earth
does_not_support: uniform amplitude at every geological site
sources.safe_use: Modern observational baseline for microseismic forcing.

sources.title: Indications of regional scale groundwater flows in the Amazon Basins: Inferences from results of geothermal studies
sources.journal_or_authority: Journal of South American Earth Sciences
supports: regional deep groundwater flow inferred from thermal and porous-media models
estimated continental-margin flow near 3287 cubic metres per second
sources.safe_use: Deep porous-media groundwater-flow hypothesis, not a conventional river.

sources.title: Aquifer systems extending far offshore on the U.S. Atlantic margin
supports: electromagnetic imaging of laterally extensive low-salinity groundwater offshore New Jersey and Massachusetts
sources.safe_use: Geophysical mapping anchor for offshore relatively fresh groundwater.

sources.title: Expedition 501 New England Shelf Hydrogeology
sources.journal_or_authority: International Ocean Drilling Programme and U.S. National Science Foundation
supports: recovery of subseafloor cores and relatively fresh water samples from the New England continental shelf
sources.safe_use: Current direct-sampling authority; final geochemical interpretation remains in progress.

sources.title: Hydrous mantle transition zone indicated by ringwoodite included within diamond
supports: local ringwoodite inclusion containing about one weight percent water-equivalent hydrogen
transition-zone minerals can store structural hydrogen
does_not_support: a liquid ocean at 410-660 km depth
quartz as the mantle water host
sources.safe_use: Deep-water storage in mineral structures, not free liquid.

sources.title: Hydrogen speciation in synthetic quartz
sources.journal_or_authority: Physics and Chemistry of Minerals / USGS record
supports: molecular water and OH impurities can occur in quartz
water can be trapped in fluid inclusions
does_not_support: bulk pure SiO2 generating water from pressure and temperature alone
sources.safe_use: Boundary between quartz framework and included or defect-bound hydrogen.

sources.title: The chemistry of habitable oceans in the Solar system
supports: planetary waters can have diverse chemistry governed by water-rock interaction and hydrodynamics
does_not_support: all rocky-planet water must have the same salinity
sources.safe_use: Planetary salinity and chemistry diversity boundary.
